controls.selectedNone: Click a star or planet (N / P to cycle)
controls.onscreenHint: Drag to look · click to identify · Shift-click to measure · scroll to zoom · ? for help

screenSummary.playArea: The play area shows a first-person planetarium view of the sky for an observer on Earth, including bright stars and solar-system bodies. Sky color follows the Sun's altitude from day through twilight to night.
screenSummary.controlArea: The control area has a collapsible panel overlaid on the sky with location, latitude, longitude, date and time presets, year/month/day/hour jump controls, play/pause, field of view, display options, and a Reset All button. Preferences → Simulation controls star names, constellation lines, planet names, and a deeper star catalog.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Drag the sky to look around, click or press N or P to select a star or planet for details, use the wheel to zoom, and press play to watch the sky rotate as time advances.

a11y.currentDetails: Latitude {{latitude}}°, looking toward azimuth {{azimuth}}° at altitude {{altitude}}°, local sidereal time {{lst}} h. The simulation is {{playState}}.
a11y.selectedAnnouncement: {{name}}, magnitude {{mag}}, right ascension {{ra}} hours, declination {{dec}} degrees, altitude {{alt}} degrees, azimuth {{az}} degrees.
a11y.measureFirstPoint: First measurement point set. Shift-click a second point to measure the angle between them.

controls.skyViewHelp: Arrow keys pan the view. Hold Control with arrows to advance civil time. N and P cycle through stars and planets in view. Escape clears the selection. The wheel changes field of view. Click a star or planet to select it.
controls.controlPanelHelpExpanded: Collapse to reduce overlay over the sky.
controls.controlPanelHelpCollapsed: Expand to show location, time, and display controls.
controls.observerLocationPanelHelpExpanded: Collapse to reduce overlay over the sky.
controls.observerLocationPanelHelpCollapsed: Expand to show the location map and latitude / longitude controls.
controls.observerLocationMapHelp: Drag the pin on the world map to set your latitude and longitude.
controls.useMyLocationHelp: Set latitude and longitude from your device, asking permission if needed.
controls.useMyLocationError: Couldn't find your location. Set latitude and longitude manually or drag the map.
controls.showEquatorialGrid: Show equatorial RA and Dec grid
controls.showAtmosphere: Show atmospheric sky colors and daytime star fade
controls.trueScaleBodies: Use true angular sizes for planet discs. The Sun and Moon are always true scale.
controls.showEcliptic: Show the ecliptic, the Sun's yearly path
controls.showObjectPath: Show the daily path of the selected object across the sky
controls.measureToolHelp: Click two points or objects to measure the angle between them. Click empty sky to clear.

keyboardHelp.lookPanDescription: Arrow keys pan azimuth and altitude. Drag with the mouse for the same motion.
keyboardHelp.advanceCivilTimeDescription: Ctrl plus left or right arrow advances or rewinds civil time. Matches Ctrl-drag.
keyboardHelp.changeFieldOfViewDescription: Scroll the mouse wheel to zoom the field of view in or out.
keyboardHelp.selectNextDescription: N selects the next named star or planet in the current view.
keyboardHelp.selectPreviousDescription: P selects the previous named star or planet in the current view.
keyboardHelp.clearSelectionDescription: Escape clears the selected star or planet.

preferences.title: Simulation
preferences.deepStarCatalog: Deeper star catalog (fainter stars to mag 7.5)
